channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Honkai: Nexus Anima ‘Evolution Test’ begins July 9
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Smartphone

Sign-ups now available.

miHoYo will host the “Evolution Test” for creature-collector adventure strategy game Honkai: Nexus Anima beginning July 9 across PC, iOS, and Android, the company announced. An end date was not announced. Sign-ups are now available via the official website.

Here is an overview of the test, via miHoYo:

As the latest entry in the Honkai series, Honkai: Nexus Anima will further expand its core gameplay and narrative content during the Evolution Test, introducing new characters, Anima, regions, and gameplay systems to offer players a richer adventure experience.

Honkai: Nexus Anima is a creature-collecting adventure strategy game in the Honkai series. In this world, Nexus refers to the unseen bonds woven between paired Aspects, such as Love and Hate, Light and Dark, and Reality and Illusion. After the Sundering, these bonds were shattered, and their remnant powers scattered across the world in the form of Anima. From this fractured order, players set out to forge bonds with Anima and harness the power of Aspects through them. These powers open up diverse battle tactics while shaping the way players explore, solve challenges, and engage with the expansive overworld. From quiet town benches and tree-lined paths to windows glowing with warm light, every place explored alongside an Anima bears witness to the unseen bonds of the Nexus. As players venture into uncharted realms, Anima stand with them in thrilling Nexus Battles and forge bonds that take root through each step into the unknown, while the truth buried within the player’s past slowly comes to light.

  • ❮
  • ❯

In the Evolution Test, players step into the role of a plane-hopping traveler who has escaped captivity and set out from Iia, a quiet town in the Reality Plane. As the journey begins, the world of Honkai: Nexus Anima opens wider, revealing new stories, distinctive gameplay, and richer paths of exploration. Across the many Planes, Anima evolution stands among the world’s most wondrous phenomena. Each transformation is more than a change in form—it is a sign of the bond taking shape, as Anima grow stronger to protect the first moment that brought them and the player together. Beyond encountering Anima and forming lasting connections with them, players can preserve that moment through a unique photo and give each companion a name of its own. On especially fortunate days, they may even cross paths with a rare Chromatic Anima, turning an unexpected encounter into a memory unlike any other.

As the starting point of the journey, Iia will reveal a fuller portrait of itself. From the lively streets of the New Quarter to its distinctive commercial spaces and architectural landmarks, its many corners gradually open up, inviting moments of exploration, quiet pause, and a closer look at the town’s unique atmosphere. Beyond its streets and landmarks, Iia holds stories waiting to be uncovered. In locations such as Nexus Hall and the True Nexus Building, key figures from different factions will come into view, their ideals and actions revealing the world’s hidden complexity. Each encounter and choice becomes another thread drawing the journey closer to its truths. Along the way, new characters such as Nanafey, Bai Mei, and Olympia will also cross paths with the traveler. Together, they will venture deeper into the unknown, following the traces of forgotten histories and seeking answers to truths long buried within the world itself.

The Evolution Test will be a limited, free closed beta test with a data wipe, available on PC, iOS, and Android. A number of players will be selected from those who have pre-registered and successfully submitted the recruitment survey, and will be granted access to the test. Further details regarding the Evolution Test will be shared in future announcements.

Watch a new trailer and gameplay video below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Evolution Teaser: “Until We Meet”

English

Japanese

Korean

Traditional Chinese

Evolution Test Gameplay Showcase

English

Japanese

Korean

Traditional Chinese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/honkai-nexus-anima-evolution-test-begins-july-9
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Blue Reflection Quartet second trailer, pre-orders now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2

Due out on July 30.

Publisher Koei Tecmo and developer Gust have released the second official trailer and opened digital pre-orders for heroic RPG collection Blue Reflection Quartet.

Here is an overview of the collection, via Koei Tecmo:

About Blue Reflection

Our tears turned the world ever so slightly blue.

The Blue Reflection series depicts the emotions and bonds of female students across a variety of mediums, with character design and series supervision by famed illustrator Mel Kishida. Blue Reflection Quartet contains all four of these emotional stories in a single collection.

New features make the series more accessible than ever before, with a more detailed look at the setting that will help newcomers understand events and provide a deeper look for returning fans.

Blue Reflection

A console game which told the story of an injured former ballerina as she used her powers as a magical Reflector to form bonds with others and fight off the looming threat to the world. Textures have been updated to high resolution versions, and new visual effects such as glistening water add to the world’s atmosphere. New features such as autosave have been included, offering a more enjoyable experience than ever before.

Blue Reflection: Ray

An anime that tells the story of magical Reflectors clashing with each other over their conflicting beliefs and emotions.This game tells a condensed version of the story, with the major characters rendered in 3D.Follow Hiori and Ruka as they explore the Common while re-experiencing their memories on their way to confront a student from another academy who is trying to recreate the world. New short stories expand on the anime’s story, and a new prologue and epilogue connect this entry to the other works in the series.

Blue Reflection: Sun

A mobile game that told the story of a team of students living in a dying world as they hold on to hope for the future.
SUN‘s story has been remade as a console game using the systems of BLUE REFLECTION: Second Light to recreate the memorable battles of the original game. 24 new character events have been added alongside the main story, and the prologue and epilogue from the original release connect this entry to the other works in the series.

Blue Reflection: Second Light

A console game that tells the story of a group of students with no memories who find themselves in a strange world. Through their adventures, they must look deep inside their hearts, regain their memories, and discover the truth about the world they are living in.

This entry in the Blue Reflection project first came out in October of 2021. This release for Blue Reflection Quartet features eight additional characters from RAY and SUN who can join you in battle. These new characters also join Yuzuki and Lime as new additions to the Photo Mode! New features such as increased battle speed, faster movement speed while exploring, and event fast-forwarding have been included, offering a more enjoyable experience than ever before.

Reference

A database that gives deeper understanding of the world of the Blue Reflection series. Here you can check chronological flowcharts of major events in the stories, as well as character relationship charts across the entire series. This in-depth overview of the characters and the series’ setting will make the games even more enjoyable!

There is also a gallery where official artwork created by Mel Kishida can be viewed.

Digital Preorder Bonus: Set of Four Special Photo Frames

Obtain the following photo frames that can be used in Blue Reflection: Second Light‘s photo mode: Blue Reflection, RAY, SUN, and Second Light.

Note:

  • This content may become available for purchase or for free separately at a later date.

Free Limited-Time Bonus: Special Photo Frame: Quartet

This photo frame can be used in Blue Reflection: Second Light‘s photo mode.

Note:

  • This content is available for free until August 12th, 2026.
  • This content may become available for purchase separately at a later date.

Blue Reflection Quartet is due out digitally for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on July 30 worldwide. In Japan, the PlayStation 5 and Switch versions will be available both physically and digitally.

Watch the trailer below.

Second Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/blue-reflection-quartet-second-trailer-pre-orders-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rose & Camellia Collection ‘Online Battle Mode’ update launches June 22
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Unleash a magnificent and intense slap duel.

The online battle mode update for slap battle action game Rose & Camellia Collection will launch on June 22, publisher PLAYISM and developer Nigoro announced.

“With the implementation of online battles, the destined slap battles against the world’s strongest players are about to begin,” PLAYISM said. “Let your elegant fighting spirit reside in your hands, and unleash a magnificent and intense slap duel!”

Rose & Camellia Collection is available now for Switch.

Watch a new trailer below.

“Online Battle Mode” Update Release Date Trailer

Japanese

Traditional Chinese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/rose-camellia-collection-online-battle-mode-update-launches-june-22
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: This Week’s Japanese Game Releases: Star Fox, Villion: Code, more
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

DELTARUNE Chapter 5, DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round, more.

Star Fox for Switch 2, and Villion: Code for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, and Switch are the highlight of this week’s Japanese video game releases.

Also due out this week are DELTARUNE Chapter 5 for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, Switch, and PC, DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC, and Medabots Card Robattle RB for Switch.

Get the full list of this week’s Japanese game releases below. It should be noted that Gematsu will receive a small percentage of money from purchases made through Play-Asia links. You can also save five percent with our multi-use “GEMATSU2025” coupon code.

Physical and Digital

  • Atomic Heart: Ultimate Edition (PS5) – Already available worldwide.
  • Blackish House ←sideZ: Retour (Switch)
    • Special Edition (Switch)
  • Kemco RPG Selection Vol. 7 (PS5)
    • Asian Version (PS5)
  • Kemco RPG Selection Vol. 14 (Switch)
    • Asian Version (Switch)
  • MAMIYA: A Shared Illusion of the World’s End (Switch) – Launching digitally worldwide.
    • Limited Edition (Switch)
    • Asian Version
      • Standard Edition (Switch)
      • Limited Edition (Switch)
  • Medabots Card Robattle RB Kabuto Ver. (Switch)
  • Medabots Card Robattle RB Kuwagata Ver. (Switch)
  • Medabots Card Robattle RB Deluxe Edition (Switch)
  • My Little Puppy (PS5, Switch) – Already available digitally worldwide.
    • Limited Edition (PS5, Switch)
    • Asian Version
      • Standard Edition (PS5, Switch)
      • Limited Edition (PS5, Switch)
  • My Merry May with be (Switch)
    • Limited Edition (Switch)
  • goHELLgo Gou: Tsukiotoshitego (PS4, Switch, PC)
    • Limited Edition (Switch, PC)
  • LOVEPICAL-POPPY! (Switch)
    • Deluxe Limited Edition (Switch)
  • Outbound (PS5, Switch 2, Switch) – Game-key card on Switch 2. Already available worldwide.
  • Promise Mascot Agency (PS5, Switch) – Already available digitally worldwide.
  • SAEKO: Giantess Dating Sim (Switch) – Launching digitally worldwide.
    • Limited Edition (Switch)
  • Star Fox (Switch 2) – Launching worldwide.
    • North American Version (Switch 2)
    • European Version (Switch 2)
    • Asian Version (Switch 2)
  • Villion: Code (PS5, Switch 2, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
    • Limited Edition (PS5, Switch 2, Switch)

Digital-Only

  • Catto’s Post Office (Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • Citizen Sleeper (Switch 2) – Launching worldwide
  • Citizen Sleeper 2: Starward Vector (Switch 2) – Launching worldwide.
  • Dark Scrolls (Switch, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Deer & Boy (PS5, Xbox Series, Switch, PC [Steam, Epic Games Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • DELTARUNE Chapter 5 (PS5, Switch 2, PS4, Switch, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Devil May Cry 5 Devil Hunter Edition (Switch 2) – Launching worldwide.
  • Dragon Quest X Online: Stray Children of Space and Time (PS4, Switch, PC)
  • Dragon Quest X Online All-In-One Package Version 1-8 (PS4, Switch, PC)
  • EMPULSE Early Access (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Flesh Made Fear (PS5) – Launching worldwide.
  • Gecko Gods (Switch 2) – Launching worldwide.
  • GHOST at DAWN (Xbox Series, Xbox One, PC [Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • LOVEPICAL-POPPY! (PS4)
  • Moldwasher (PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Mousebusters (Xbox One, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • My Merry May with be (PS4)
  • NAMCO LEGENDARY Mountains (Switch 2, Switch, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Villion: Code (PS4) – Launching worldwide.
  • Wanderstop (Switch 2, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • YinYang Street: Separate Ways (Switch)
  • YinYang Street Ultimate Edition (PS5)

The PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Switch titles listed above can be purchased through Japanese PlayStation Network and Nintendo accounts using prepaid cards:

  • PlayStation Store cards (yen): 1,100 / 3,000 / 5,000 / 10,000
  • Nintendo eShop cards (yen): 1,000 / 1,500 / 3,000 / 5,000 / 9,000

Additionally, direct top-ups for gacha games are available here, including:

  • Blue Protocol: Star Resonance
  • Genshin Impact
  • Honkai: Star Rail
  • Love and Deepspace
  • Marvel Rivals
  • Where Winds Meet
  • Zenless Zone Zero

Demos, DLC, Updates, etc.

  • Gecko Gods “Forgotten Cove” Update (PS5, Switch 2, Switch, PC)


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/this-weeks-japanese-game-releases-star-fox-villion-code-more
